items.rationale: Seen as crypto's clearest product-market fit and the first credible path to onboard a billion users; cheap/instant dollar transfers, B2B settlement, remittances, AI-agent payments. GENIUS Act signing (2025-07-18) cemented house conviction. Volume uncorrelated with trading = organic demand.
items.age_note: building since 2024-08 ('killer app'), peaked mid-2025 with GENIUS Act

citations.quote: Stablecoins now present what I believe is the first credible opportunity to onboard a billion people into crypto.
Stablecoins have found product market fit and this chart shows it.
Stablecoins are the "killer app" of crypto, and here's a bunch of great data that shows it.

items.rationale: Eddy Lazzarin's signature thesis — crypto networks as the native antidote to physical-infrastructure monopolies; user-generated services for the physical world. Backed with the Daylight (energy) investment and a defined framework (networks that can't be computationally verified).

citations.quote: DePIN is eating the world
DePIN provides the most salient path towards making atoms look more like bits for builders and investors alike.
The future of consumer crypto? DePIN

items.rationale: Most active recent investment cluster: Catena (agent payments), Yupp (AI eval data), Poseidon (training data), Ambient (useful PoW inference), World (proof of human). Thesis: AI agents need a native economy and crypto provides trust, identity, and rails machines can respect.

citations.quote: Machine-speed systems need machine-speed money. Catena is building the missing link, and we're excited to back them.
AI needs strong, reliable evaluation based on large-scale human input. Crypto is the trust machine that can help deliver it.
This time is no different: AI needs blockchain-enabled computing.

items.rationale: Held as the endgame for scaling and verifiability; Justin Thaler's Jolt advances (sum-check/lookups). Strong technical conviction, but now explicitly tempered: still 'not ready for prime time' — performance overhead >100,000x and pervasive bugs.

citations.quote: I still think ZK is under hyped. Let's talk about why
while SNARKs and zkVMs show immense promise, they're not ready for complex, high-stakes deployments. Bugs are everywhere

items.rationale: Privacy reframed as the next key bottleneck now that infra/regulation are maturing — Seismic, Miden, Aztec backed; argues radical privacy should be the default across the financial stack. Distinguishes 'trusted' (TEE) from 'trustless'.

citations.quote: We have the technology to make radical privacy the default. Let's build it.
Privacy is essential, and I'm surprised to see this take coming from the OG collaborative proofs founder.

items.rationale: Scott Kominers / Chris Dixon thesis: generative AI breaks the creator-platform covenant; blockchains provide provenance, attribution, licensing. Story Protocol ($80M) and Poseidon back it. 'IP becomes valueless without these tools.'

citations.quote: I don't think I've ever seen an infrastructure innovation that excites me as much as @StoryProtocol.
The potential for abundance does not mean IP becomes valueless

items.rationale: Viewed as popular and growing; now unlocked by stablecoin/market-structure clarity and UX simplification (Legend, Universal). The 'lawyer-led product design' era is ending, freeing DeFi to add real value accrual.

citations.quote: the absurdities of that approach are no longer necessary... DeFi stands to benefit from that shift
DeFi remains popular — and it's growing

items.rationale: Open protocols (Farcaster, XMTP) as antidote to corporate-network capture; interoperability and data ownership. Long-running Dixon thesis, with fresh XMTP (quantum-resistant messaging) emphasis.

citations.quote: The time is now for no compromises fast, secure, decentralized messaging with seamlessly integrated apps and payments.
The Decentralized Social experiment is underway, and vibrant developer ecosystems are emerging around open protocols like Farcaster.

items.rationale: Ali Yahya's 'two-sided marketplace for cryptoeconomic security' — likened to Ethereum's smart-contract unlock for 100x faster infra innovation. Conviction shown via repeated capital ($70M token follow-on).

citations.quote: Over the past couple years, the Eigen team has unlocked a whole new paradigm for building applications that are verifiable and credibly neutral.
EigenLayer is the verifiable cloud. Massive leap forward today toward the realization of that vision.

items.rationale: Consistently the house's #1 cultural concern: zero intrinsic value, attracts regulators' ire, deters serious founders, and damages industry credibility. Framed as gambling, not value creation. Tolerated as legal but seen as net-negative externality.

citations.quote: Meme coins are just the next evolution of the gambling industry.
we shouldn't pretend that the casino doesn't set us back. Deterring great founders and giving ammo to politicized regulators

items.rationale: Miles Jennings' core worry: tokens whose value derives from a controlled company (archetype FTT) being passed off as decentralized network tokens. Excluding these from securities law would create a 'Walmart/AWS Token' loophole that damages capital markets.

citations.quote: calls for market structure legislation that ignore decentralization are calls to create a significant loophole in securities laws
If you imbue a network token with all the trust dependencies that exist with ordinary securities, then that token should be subject to securities laws.

items.rationale: Foundations used as a shortcut to fake decentralization are now seen as unnecessary, extractive, operationally crippling, and tax-risky given improving US rules. Push toward DUNA/BORG/PBC structures instead.
items.age_note: newly prominent 2025-06; reversal from acceptance as necessary evil

citations.quote: It's time for crypto move on from the "foundation" model.
some consultants are manufacturing a sense of urgency to push them into foreign foundations... All the reasons they give... are nonsense.

items.rationale: Eddy raises unresolved structural concerns for less-mature-asset treasury vehicles: PubCo disclosure conflicts, Gary Plastic issues, trading below NAV. Manageable for BTC/ETH/SOL, doubtful where insiders spin up vehicles for their own volatile tokens.

citations.quote: I'm not sure these problems are solvable where management teams of less mature projects are spinning up and controlling these public vehicles themselves.

items.rationale: Despite enthusiasm for ZK, the team flags security as the gravest threat: circuits riddled with bugs, protection currently by obscurity, formal verification years away. A caution attached to the same tech they champion.

citations.quote: the security issues cannot be overstated I think they are the biggest threat to snarks not fulfilling their potential
Until we reach Stage 2, we can't really call a zkVM "secure"

items.rationale: Warns that treating trusted execution environments as equivalent to trustless systems will cause real harm; defense-in-depth (proofs for integrity, enclaves for privacy) but not a substitute for trustlessness.

citations.quote: The conflation of trusted execution environments with trustless blockchains is going to lead to a lot of pain.

items.rationale: Live tactical worry: the Senate's simplified 'ancillary asset' construct opens ambiguities and SEC-discretion loopholes that the House CLARITY Act resolves; risks tokens being reclassified as notes/profits interests.

citations.quote: the draft's foundational "ancillary asset" construct currently opens up serious ambiguities that CLARITY resolves.

items.rationale: Research-driven concern that PBS decentralizes validation but centralizes block-building via order-flow feedback loops ('proof of MEV'); multi-block/cross-chain MEV could worsen it. Caution against rushing to regulate MEV, though.
items.age_note: research from 2024-10; not restated as a top theme since

citations.quote: PBS is reducing centralization in validation, but might create centralization in building because of this... proof of MEV competition

items.rationale: Recurring market-structure worry: easy early liquidity for insiders breeds get-rich-quick schemes and retail harm (SPAC analogy). House prefers 4yr/1yr lockups tied to decentralization milestones; disclosure alone is insufficient.

citations.quote: Unfettered access to secondary market liquidity is an existential threat to innovation.
4 year/1 year cliff token lockups should be standard.

items.rationale: Canonical 'network token': value accrues via protocol fees and the EIP-1559 burn (deflationary), decentralized so not a security. Defended as economically a marketplace-ownership asset, not a commodity or security.

citations.quote: The network token is just ownership in the network/marketplace that sells it.
one would have to assume that the SEC now believes ETH is a security... which obviously isn't the case.

items.rationale: Praised for pragmatic, ship-focused engineering culture driving years of growth; high-throughput chain anchoring stablecoin/payments and DeFi growth. Multiple metrics show Solana leading active addresses.

citations.quote: shipping beats maximalism
Maybe the best description I've seen for why Solana has grown so much over the last few years

items.rationale: Respected as the most secure/decentralized network and digital-gold store of value, distinct from memecoins despite lacking cash flows; 'it's incredible that we have bitcoin.' Note caution about long-run fee-vs-block-reward security.

citations.quote: being the native asset of what is arguably the most secure and decentralized network gives it economic characteristics... unlike any memecoin.
It's incredible that we have bitcoin.

items.rationale: Repeated capital conviction ($70M token follow-on with lockup) in EigenLayer's verifiable, credibly-neutral application paradigm.

citations.quote: we've invested an additional $70M in @eigencloud tokens (EIGEN) with a lockup.

items.rationale: $55M token investment with 3-year lockup; protocol seen enabling new businesses and complex workflows moving onchain.

citations.quote: we've invested an additional $55M in @LayerZero_Core tokens (ZRO), with a 3-year lockup.

items.rationale: Used as the clean example of a network token that can capture protocol fees (via mechanisms like UniStaker) without being a company-backed security.

citations.quote: UNI can capture fees from the network (the switch is off right now) via a mechanism like UniStaker.

items.rationale: The recurring archetype of a token that is economically a profits interest in a company (FTX) and should have been regulated as a security; cited dozens of times as the cautionary boundary case for network-vs-company tokens.
items.age_note: used as defunct cautionary archetype throughout 2025

citations.quote: FTT was a security. If you have an argument as to why it wasn't, I'm all ears.
FTT (whose value was solely derived from a centralized company, FTX)

items.rationale: Treated as inherently valueless, purely speculative, redistributive rather than value-creating; a regulatory and reputational liability. TRUMP grouped with these in token-taxonomy work.

citations.quote: True memecoins have no intrinsic value (no utility, no company, no anything) and their price is driven purely by speculation
memecoins have no inherent value. That's the whole point.

risk_regime.rationale: As of mid-2025 the house is at peak optimism: GENIUS Act signed into law (2025-07-18), CLARITY Act advancing with bipartisan support, control-based decentralization framework gaining consensus, institutional adoption (ETPs, Circle IPO, Stripe/Visa/Mastercard), stablecoin volume at all-time highs, and infra finally 'ready for apps.' Repeatedly says 'never been a better time to build' and pivots from defense to offense ('sales era'). This is a sharp reversal from the 2024 risk-off, lawfare-defensive posture under the prior SEC. Residual caution lives in execution risk on pending legislation, ZK/TEE security maturity, and speculative excess (memecoins, treasury vehicles) — but the dominant, most-recent signal is decisively constructive.

notes: Biggest recency signal is a REGIME REVERSAL: through 2024 the team's overriding posture was defensive/risk-off, dominated by SEC 'lawfare,' debanking, and regulatory uncertainty (top concern by volume). After the US election and 2025 policy shift, that concern was largely resolved into optimism; report the current stance (risk_on) and treat regulatory uncertainty as a reversed prior, not a live top concern — its residue survives only as tactical worry over legislative drafting (Senate ancillary-asset construct, 2025-07-31). Citations: 'There has never been a better time to start a project in crypto' (2025-01-08); 'I've never been more optimistic about the chances of market structure legislation passing in the U.S.' (2025-06-11). Corpus is multi-author (Dixon=networks/ownership; Jennings=legal/decentralization/token-classification; Lazzarin=DePIN/ZK/infra; Kominers=NFTs/IP/market-design; Yahya=EigenLayer; Thaler/Ragsdale=ZK/Jolt; Matsuoka=metrics) — 'consensus' here is a synthesis of broadly aligned but individually-voiced views; sharpest internal nuance is ZK (simultaneously 'underhyped' and 'not ready for prime time'). A persistent throughline across all authors and all dates: decentralization defined as ABSENCE OF CONTROL is the organizing principle separating what they champion (network tokens, open infra) from what they disdain (memecoins, company-backed tokens, foundation theater). One taxonomy note (2025-07-22): Eddy proposed dropping the word 'decentralization' for concrete goals (censorship resistance, composability, eliminating platform risk) — a framing shift, not a change in conviction.
